defs: A kind of knot often used at the end of a rope; a wall knot; a wale.

records.ety: [AS. weall, from L. vallum a wall, vallus a stake, pale, palisade; akin to Gr. <?/ a nail. Cf. Interval.]

defs: A work or structure of stone, brick, or other materials, raised to some height, and intended for defense or security, solid and permanent inclosing fence, as around a field, a park, a town, etc., also, one of the upright inclosing parts of a building or a room.
quotes: The plaster of the wall of the King's palace.

defs: A defense; a rampart; a means of protection; in the plural, fortifications, in general; works for defense.
quotes: The waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
In such a night, Troilus, methinks, mounted the Troyan walls.
To rush undaunted to defend the walls.

defs: An inclosing part of a receptacle or vessel; as, the walls of a steam-engine cylinder.
The side of a level or drift.
The country rock bounding a vein laterally.
notes: ☞ Wall is often used adjectively, and also in the formation of compounds, usually of obvious signification; as in wall paper, or wall-paper; wall fruit, or wall-fruit; wallflower, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Walled (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Walling.]

defs: To inclose with a wall, or as with a wall.
quotes: The king of Thebes, Amphion, That with his singing walled that city.

defs: To defend by walls, or as if by walls; to fortify.
quotes: The terror of his name that walls us in.

defs: To close or fill with a wall, as a doorway.
